data.reasoning: Both models skipped reviewing the PKGBUILD due to incorrect binary file classification. Without examining the PKGBUILD (which controls build/install execution), safety cannot be determined.
coverage_issues: Critical file PKGBUILD was skipped by both models due to false binary detection
Potential .install scripts or build hooks were not reviewed as the audit halted at PKGBUILD failure
false_negatives: All build/install risks were missed since PKGBUILD wasn't reviewed
Potential malicious commands in build()/package() functions remain undetected
Network calls or filesystem operations in PKGBUILD were not examined
learnings: Improve file type detection: Never skip PKGBUILD based on binary classification
Prioritize critical files: PKGBUILD must be reviewed first before any other files
Add fallback mechanism: If PKGBUILD fails initial read, attempt hexdump/strings analysis
Enhance error handling: Audit should proceed to other critical files after PKGBUILD failure
